entry.title: Re: Inbound Idoc is also getting generated with outbound Idoc
entry.content: Issue is resolved.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

I'm probably late on the uptake here but would like to start an application for the Beginner Digital Skills Initiative.  The following are reasons I'd qualify:

  • Women
  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
  • First generation college students (already have degree though for many years)
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
  • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves (currently between work and not finding anything)

Please let me know next steps as I look forward to receiving a registration link.  As well, what would you recommend as the most expeditious path considering a late application?  

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Integration of Third Party (Non-SAP) systems with Central Finance
entry.content:
Hi Vinay,

This is a very good article.  I am currently working on a scenario where we have few non-SAP systems, one of them is PeopleSoft. Can we use Oracle SOA for data extraction, SLT for transforming data and moving to staging table and eventually replicating to CFIN? Which third party tool is cost effective and works better in this case? 

1. Non-SAP ERP-->Magnitude-->Data Services for transforming-->SLT-->CFIN

2. Non-SAP ERP-->Syniti-->SLT(transforming and replication-->CFIN

3. Non-SAP ERP-->SDI-->CFIN

4. Non-SAP ERP --> Data Sphere--> SLT--> CFIN

5. Non-SAP ERP --> Oracle SOA-->SLT for transformation and replication-->CFIN

Thank you

SSR

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative - 2024
entry.content: I am from Nicaragua,Central America. i am Electronic engineer and I had the opportunity to work with Sap. I woul like to obtain my certification.

entry.title: Re: How to register delivery dates for supplier responses...
entry.content: Thank you for your reply. The Help Portal has a description of "Data Exchange Using Excel." https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/4fd9e2570892f232e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US&version=2402.504 I thought it might be possible to update all at once using this method, but I don't know the actual steps (such as which app to use). Do you know how to use "Data Exchange Using Excel"?

entry.title: Re: Unable to perform POST from SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
As @miroll suggested, the blog contains the answer.

You just need to add the code below inside in your package.json

"cds": {
  "features":{
     "odata_new_adapter": false
  }
}

entry.title: Re: SAP PE Session 'Conversion to SAP S/4 HANA and RISE with SAP - in one Go!' (September 19, 2024)
entry.content:
Hi, 

I wanted to attend this session but couldn't find the meeting link in the invite. Can someone help how to attend these sessions? Also past recordings? Thanks

entry.title: Re: Goods Movement Types
entry.content:
Sorry,It's not helpful to my question.

Here are my requirements:

While developing the API, I found that to perform stock transfers, the GoodsMovementTypefield needs to be filled in, representing the transfer code. For example, if I want to transfer material from "Quality Inspection Stock" to "Unrestricted-Use Stock," the GoodsMovementType = 321.

So far, there are many codes that I am unaware of, such as transferring from "Blocked Stock" to "Returns blocked stock" or from "Unrestricted-Use Stock" to "Returns blocked stock". These codes should ideally be documented and available for reference when developing APIs. Could you please provide me with the relevant documentation?

entry.title: how to open a connection from S4/HANA to other database
entry.content:
how to open a connection from S4/HANA to oracle.

we are in the process to setup s4/HANA and we need to be able:

  1.  to connect from s4/HANA to another database (oracle on premise)
  2. We need to connect from on premise oracle database to s4/HANA

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: how to open a connection from S4/HANA to other database
entry.content:
Dear customer,

You can utilize SDA(Smart Data Access) feature provided by SAP HANA.
Please refer to the following information.

  • SDA feature can be used for this requirement.
    • 2180119 - FAQ: SAP HANA Smart Data Access
      2600176 - SAP HANA Smart Data Access Supported Remote Sources
    • SAP HANA Smart Data Access 

      • SAP HANA smart data access lets you access remote data as if the data were stored in local tables in SAP HANA, without copying the data into SAP HANA. 
        This capability lets you access and integrate data from multiple remote systems in real time.

        In SAP HANA, you use linked database or create virtual tables, which point to remote tables in different remote databases, and then write SQL queries in SAP HANA that use these virtual tables. 
        The SAP HANA query processor optimizes these queries by executing the relevant part of the query in the remote database, returning the results of the query to SAP HANA, and then completing the operation. 
        Physical data movement is not supported by SAP HANA smart data access.

        Because smart data access is part of the SAP HANA core system, no additional licensing is required to use it. 
        Additional support packages and patches are available for download from the SAP Software Download Center, and are installed using the SAP HANA database lifecyle manager (HDBLCM).

Regarding to the reverse connection(Oracle -> HANA), please open a support ticket to ORACLE support team.
Hope this helps.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Smart Data Streaming - File/Hadoop Json - Array Issue
entry.content: Thank you for clarifying the previous points. I have a question regarding importing a JSON array into an input stream. I’ve started a new thread here: Smart Data Streaming Kafka Adapter - Importing JSON Array. I would appreciate it if you could take a look. Additionally, thank you for informing me about the upcoming end of maintenance for SAP. Could you please suggest alternative tools for streaming analytics?

entry.title: Re: Smart Data Streaming - File/Hadoop Json - Array Issue
entry.content: Hello @RobertWaywell I’ve started a new thread here:https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/smart-data-streaming-kafka-adapter-importing-json-array/qaq-p/13870708 . Please have a look.

entry.title: Re: New features in S/4 HANA PS
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition contains a brand new Professional Services functionality that goes beyond on managing commercial projects.  This was exclusively introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, so there are many deltas and additional functions compare with the Commercial Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition / SAP S/4HANA on-premise and ECC.

To get an idea of what is included in this area, please check this link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/5d19f357eb96be12e10000000a4450e5.html

Hope this helps,

Marco

entry.title: Re: Duplicate check functionality for Accounting Documents

You can try to write the checking logic in T-code：GGB0

entry.title: Re: Unable to perform POST from SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
Thanks @miroll , the blog contains the answer

This works code below inside in your package.json

"cds": {
  "features":{
     "odata_new_adapter": false
  }
}

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I am Prasad from Malaysia. Recently I got exposed with SAP and would like to pursue further. The free certification would help me to kickstart my career and for following mentioned reasons as well:

  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
  • First generation college students
  • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I hope I will be selected for this initiative as I am eager to pursue careers related to SAP.

Looking forward to hear from you soon. Thank you.

Best Regards,

Prasad

entry.title: プロフェッショナルサービスで、得意先プロジェクトで請求機能を非アクティブで利用したい
entry.content:
ご担当者様

いつもお世話になっております。

S4HANA　PUBLIC　CLOUDにてプロフェッショナルサービスを利用を考えています。

ただ会計（財務会計・管理会計）・プロフェッショナルサービスしか導入しておりません。

今回、工事管理を管理したいが原価や請求機能は外部システムにある場合、

得意先プロジェクトを使用して、プロジェクト管理できるでしょうか？

ご教示いただけますでしょうか。

 

よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: プロフェッショナルサービスで、得意先プロジェクトで請求機能を非アクティブで利用したい
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Please take a look at the following scope item regarding customer project management.

Process Navigator - SAP for Me

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: SIGNAL widget - stacked bar chart
entry.content:
Hi braintrust,

I'd appreciate help with a widget in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. I have a dataset relating to IT incidents covering 6 months. These IT incidents have, among others two case attributes:

  1. "incident category", and
  2. "incident resolved".

Incident resolved is a timestamp.

I also have a event called "Incident is resolved", which uses "incident resolved" as c_time.

I'd like to visualise in a stacked columns (stacked bar chart) incidents resolved in the respective month broken down by incident category. See the screenshot for what I'm looking for.

What would be the SIGNAL script to achieve this? I look forward to your suggestions. Thanks.

Kind regards,

 

Vladimir

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content: Hi, Is there a way to attend these events virtually?

entry.title: SAP Business One export report to Excel defaults to ".txt" instead of Excel
entry.content: SAP Business One version 10 HANA, when tried export reports to Excel, it always defaults file type as ".txt" instead of Excel, users have to manually change it to Excel file type. Is there anyway to change the default to Excel file type?

entry.title: SuccessFactors Certification (EC Payroll)
entry.content:
Professionally, I have gained valuable experience working as a Payroll Administrator at MNCs. In this role, I have been responsible for maintaining employees' data, managing organizations, and managing payroll. I have experience using SAP ECC and SuccessFactors as an end user. Overall, with 10 years of SAP experience (HCM Module) I have planned to switch to SAP functional consultant.

The 10 years of SAP end-user experience will help me to transform my career journey.

entry.title: Re: Details of Write/Read Options and Value - Calling LDA...

entry.title: How to use Account value to PREVIOUS function of Date in Data action
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement where the user wants to input the number of months for delay in payment.

Let’s say we have 3 GLs – Number_of_Month, Payment, Delayed_payment

Number_of_Month – User inputs number of months

Delayed_payment – Previous (Number_of_Month) of Payment

When I write the DA for this like below it is working for a single company code but when I have Number_of_Month for another company code then it sums up the Number_of_Month

 

INTEGER @A

@A = RESULTLOOKUP([d/GLACCOUNT] = " Number_of_Month, [d/Date] = "000000")

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/GLACCOUNT] = """ Number_of_Month, [d/Date] = "000000"> 0 THEN

DATA([d/GLACCOUNT] = "Delayed_payment") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Date] = PREVIOUS (@A, "MONTH"), [d/GLACCOUNT] = "Payment")

ENDIF

I run this for 202412 period

This works as expected when we have value for only one company code.

GL Account

Company Code

Date

Amount

Payment

CC1

202212

100

Payment

CC1

202312

200

Payment

CC2

202212

111

Payment

CC2

202312

222

Number_of_Month

CC1

#

12

Number_of_Month

CC2

#

-

Delayed_payment

CC1

202412

200

Delayed_payment

CC2

202412

-

 

But when I have months for another Company code it sums up (12+12=24 month) and shows the 202212th value for both Company codes

GL Account

Company Code

Date

Amount

Payment

CC1

202212

100

Payment

CC1

202312

200

Payment

CC2

202212

111

Payment

CC2

202312

222

Number_of_Month

CC1

#

12

Number_of_Month

CC2

#

12

Delayed_payment

CC1

202412

100

Delayed_payment

CC2

202412

111

 

I have tried using FOREACH and also VARIABLEMEMBER as well but no luck.

Is there any other way to achieve this.

Thanks in advance.

Vijay

entry.title: Re: Query manager yellow arrow for docnum from QIPF
entry.content: Share your query, please

entry.title: SAP BusinessAI – Overview for all !!!
entry.content:
SAP Business AI – Overview

While AI is taking over the market with its capabilities, every customer is keen on looking at the portfolio to improve the business and benefit from AI capabilities. SAP Business AI (Artificial Intelligence) is the suite of AI capabilities integrated into SAP's Enterprise Software Solutions. These AI functionalities are designed to help businesses automate processes, gain insights from data, improve decision-making, and enhance customer experiences.

Here's a high-level overview of what SAP Business AI encompasses:

SAP provides a unique approach to our customers for all the SAP Solutions, we have multiple AI Services options to choose from. With these options, you must have heard about our Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible approach to AI.

Most of the time, the common question that I hear is how do you offer these services and how can customers benefit from them?

As shown in the above image, we approach them mainly into 3 different buckets –

  • Embedded AI Capabilities
  • Joule
  • AI Services in SAP BTP

1. Embedded AI Capabilities:

As the name indicates, Embedded AI services are integrated directly into your enterprise applications which can be activated without the need of additional setup or new systems. You can benefit from the services instantly and use them for your business needs.

Here are some of the capabilities that you may be interested and you can find the SAP Business AI - Roadmap Explorer here.

I would like to call out SAP SuccessFactors Generative AI Services as one of the most used embedded services, you can find the availability and the data center support list here and I recommend checking the other Line of Business services based on your interest.

Next question, how do we get access to these AI services?

This can be accessed via a subscription option where you need to purchase SAP AI Units standalone or part of bundle packages. AI units can be considered as a virtual currency that can be used to consume any of the Business AI service capabilities and this will be metered against your consumption and charged accordingly. You can check the AI Services list here and you can find the Estimator Tool which can help you assess the required AI Units for your business context.

Once you purchase your required AI Units, it will be a pool of AI units that can be used by any premium AI services based on your system availability and activations. Each AI service comes with a metric that can be calculated using the Estimator Tool and your consumption will be visible on https://me.sap.com every month, and they are based on annual consumption based on the contract start date.

For more questions and support, I recommend reaching your SAP Account Executive or your Customer Success Partner to start your discussion.

2. Joule

Joule is our next-generation AI Copilot by SAP which can assist you with business-related questions and tasks. While Joule is integrated into your SAP services, it can interact with humans naturally in a contextual manner which brings multiple advantages for business users and helps to increase efficiency.  

Joule interactions are achieved using advanced algorithms and large language models (LLM) for user queries and user intents. If a user posts a question to Joule, the copilot understands the intent and with the help of the integration with your SAP Business Applications, it gives the appropriate response.

While Joule is evolving, we have expanded our scope to support user queries from SAP Business Applications, SAP Help portal, and Customer Documents e.g., Leave Policy, Company Policy, Vendor Policy, Travel Policy, Contract Policy, Reimbursement Policy, Global/Local Policy, etc...

2.1 Joule Capabilities

Joule Capabilities otherwise also called interaction patterns, we have carefully considered the most common scenarios in which a user can interact with Joule and categorize into different patterns. Each of these patterns is offered with products based on the release dates and product roadmaps. Today we support 3 different patterns, mainly

  • The transactional pattern provides our users a direct entry point to SAP the backend system. Triggering and influencing business processes with the power of natural language and generative AI. E.g. purchase orders which need to be reviewed and approved, job positions which are created, OR any other CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) based interactions. All our Cloud products are currently developing such content packages for Joule for the most relevant user interactions.
  • The navigational pattern helps our users handle business processes themselves in the relevant SAP screen. Joule allows users to navigate directly where they want to go. This is especially helpful for users who are not very familiar with navigating SAP applications.
  • The informational pattern we are providing our users knowledge-based results. These are for example policy related questions. We differ between two categories of informational Joule questions:
    • One is based on SAP-owned content to have all our knowledge base available via Joule, e.g. with the SAP-help documentation. This is managed by SAP and offered to every Joule user out of the box.
    • Additionally, we are enabling customers to upload customer-owned content. This allows customers to upload their HR policies, travel policies, information content, and others. We call this functionality Document Grounding.

We are also working the 4th the pattern

  • Analytical pattern, we plan to introduce analytical interactions. Coming later in 2024 we plan to enable analytical interactions based on JustAsk and SAP Analytics Cloud, enabling customers to leverage Joule for analytical queries. In 2025, we plan to integrate Joule into SAP Analytics Cloud and further evolve the analytical capabilities of Joule.

With the above release, we call your attention to the following services, released and plan to release to support our SAP Customers and an opportunity for Partners to do more with GenAI.

2.2 Joule Architecture

Joule provides an embedded co-pilot experience across solution areas, consistent and with memory of interactions across multiple products.

The users start with their queries in the natively integrated Joule client embedded in our respective SAP cloud applications:

Each incoming request is processed and orchestrated based on what is available.

Joule considers 3 categories:

  1. Joule analyzes if the user-prompt is relevant for Joule’s Scenario Catalog. This catalog contains metadata of all available scenarios, functions, and skills of SAP cloud applications.
  2. Joule conducts an informational filtering based on the Knowledge Catalog. This contains SAP knowledge as well as customer-owned knowledge. This process is based on Retrieval Augmented Generation for enterprise (RAGe) and lets the LLM generate an answer based and grounded on the dedicated text that was previously retrieved.
  3. Joule is aware of the user’s context and history. This includes information about which SAP cloud application the respective user is using and which additional SAP application the customer has licensed and has Joule activated. Also, Joule is aware of the user's role(s) and permissions. This means a user cannot access information or adjust business processes they couldn’t do directly in the SAP cloud application without using Joule. At last, Joule understands a user’s chat history and context, ensuring the best possible answers in the context of questions asked shortly before.

Joule takes all this contextual information and provides a much richer query to the LLM which is responsible for the dialog management. This LLM is based on a rich ecosystem of technology partner LLM offerings also available directly via SAP Business Technology Platform’s AI Foundation and the SAP Generative AI Hub. Here we are leveraging the latest LLMs and innovations available in the market under strict contractual agreements with SAP’s partners which prohibit them from training any of their AI-models on any customer data.

Next, the LLM provides a grounded response back to the Joule service or identifies the need to leverage Joule functions which includes the Joule scenarios of our SAP Cloud Applications.

  • In this case, Joule calls the respective SAP backend system to proceed with the requested query, e.g. requesting time off.
  • The response is then filtered as appropriate, and the customer gets a response knowing the conversation and output has been processed all with enterprise security, data privacy, and responsible AI considered.

2.3 Joule Services

  • SAP Document Grounding (Policy Documents) - We offer SAP Document Grounding (RAG) capability out of the box which helps Joule to comprehend content from PDF and MS Word documents. With this support, you can upload any document related to policies and processes in your SharePoint. This setup is going to be common to your SAP products. 
  • Joule for Consultants – SAP Business AI in Consulting Capability for Joule enables Joule users to tap into SAP Content like SAP Learning, SAP Help, SAP Community, and developer tutorials content. The solution empowers you to receive fast and detailed answers to questions concerning the design, implementation, or update of SAP systems - across the SAP product portfolio. You can quickly watch the demo here.
  • Joule for Developers (SAP Build Code) – SAP Build Code, generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, optimized for Java and JavaScript application development, providing a turn-key environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. You can find more details here.
  • Joule Extensibility – Gives our Customers and Partners an option to build on top of SAP standard support that we offer for Joule, planned release Q1, 2025.
  • Joule with SAP Business Technology Platform – For all your SAP BTP Admin tasks we introduce Joule in your SAP BTP Cockpit, planned Release Q4, 2024.
  • Joule + Microsoft Copilot – This is one of the amazing features of integration with Joule - SAP Business Data and Microsoft services. You can watch the demo to understand more here.

You can also find our release plan here:

2.3 Joule Pricing

With a wide variety of integrations and support offered by Joule, the important question is how I get Joule and how is it going to be charged. As a customer, you need to opt for a Joule Embedded License, an entitlement that will be added to your SAP Business Technology Platform - global account. SAP offers free and fair usage of messages based on your existing license for every LOB where Joule is supported. As an example, SAP SuccessFactors offers free messages as follows:

 

SFSF UserMessages/year
1-5,000240,000
5,001-25,0001,20,000
25,001-75,0006,000,000
>75,0009,600,000

Similar messages are added to your Joule consumption (based on your product licenses that support Joule integration). If you are using any of the GenAI services or exceeding your Joule message allocations, it will consume the SAP AI Units. For further details, you can refer to the SAP AI Service list and AI Terms.

With this, we understand the questions on prerequisites and the setup in BTP that you may have as a customer or if you are a partner helping customers make the decisions. We take this topic of Unified Joule or One Joule to our next blog to address some of the common questions, please continue reading here.

 

For additional information on SAP Joule, and how to activate the services you may look at the SAP Learning Journey.

This release will allow all our SAP SuccessFactors customers to use Joule irrespective of the SAP SuccessFactors Data Center and pick any/nearest data center where Joule is supported. You can find more updates here: SAP Note: 3519499 and What’s new with Joule.

3. SAP Business AI Services in SAP BTP

This offering is best suited while you have the use cases that you would like to achieve. The AI Services offered on the SAP Business Technology Platform give you options to integrate, Automate, and make use of the most Generative AI capabilities. These options are possible using the SAP AI Core and AI Launchpad services.

The image below can help to add more details on the GenAI Capabilities:

For further details, I recommend the blogs below:

  • How SAP's Generative AI Architecture Redefines Business Applications
  • How SAP’s Generative AI Hub facilitates embedded, trustworthy, and reliable AI
  • Generative AI – Roadmap
  • Tutorials - Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad

SAP also offers the following Business AI services, Document Information Extraction, Data Attribute Extraction, Data Attribute Recommendation, Personalized Recommendation, etc...

SAP also has AI Developer services such as SAP Build Code,  SAP ABAP capabilities (planned release), and SAP iFlow (integration flow) using GenAI.

Most of the details are available on SAP Discovery Center and some of these services are available as SAP BTP Try & Buy.

For a quick check on Generative AI Models Availability here - 3437766

Stay tuned for more updates from SAP on – Joule Extensibility Capabilities, Joule for Consultants, AI Agents, Orchestration Workflow, and more.

Quick reference can be to SAP TechEd 2024, which can help with the planned releases:

 

 

Consuming the AI Services for your Business usage

As a customer, you would be looking for information on how to consume these services. As mentioned, above these services require respective licenses:

For Embedded AI capabilities - delivered out of the box by SAP as a customer you will need AI Units and may require you to activate a check box flag to start using the services. Example, 

  • SAP SFSF 
    • Job Description Enhancement Using AI
    • Premium AI Features in SAP SuccessFactors
  • SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
  • SAP Business Networks

On the other hand, Joule and AI Services on BTP are available in a few data centers across the globe, however, they can be cross-consumed. For BTP AI services you can always refer to SAP Discovery Center – AI Solutions and Joule data center list.

Note: SAP is working on expanding the data center support on these topics, however, we do not have defined timelines. In case you plan to activate the AI services today, with available data centers, please keep in mind you may have to migrate/reactivate with new data centers, in case you would like to choose the nearest location.

With this in context, I would like to talk about factors to consider during cross-consumption of the applications:

  • DC availability
  • Latency across DCs with your SAP Cloud Applications and your SAP AI Services
  • Country / Regional Laws
  • Customer Data preference (e.g. EU Access)

To discuss the above topics, we can take examples with a few scenarios.

Scenario 1

The customer has a single SAP product with a data center in his country and has an AI data center available in his country. In this case, the customer can choose the supported data center in his country and activate the required service.


Scenario 2

The customer has a single SAP product with a data center in his country but has no AI data center available in his country. In this case, the customer can choose the nearest AI data center and activate the service.


Scenario 3

The customer has multiple SAP products that are in the same country and has an AI data center available in his country. In this case, the customer can choose the AI data center in his country which could be nearest to all SAP products that are licensed.


Scenario 4

The customer has multiple SAP products that are in different locations and has no AI data center available in his country. In this case, the customer can choose the nearest AI data center available and activate the services.

Let me put this with a few examples, your scenarios can be based on your different systems at different locations. To help you choose your AI data center, I can take a few cases with Joule.

Case 1: You plan to integrate a single Joule application with SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, SAP Integrated Business Planning, and SAP Product Lifecycle Management that are in different data centers, you can choose the region for Joule that is close to all the application datacenters.

 

Case 2:  You plan the Joule integration with SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud, SAP Integrated Business Planning, and SAP Product Lifecycle Management with different data centers in different regions, you can prioritize the SAP Systems where there are maximum business users, and you can choose the appropriate SAP BTP Subaccount.

Based on your priority of SAP SFSF - Virginia and S/4 HANA – Toronto and IBP and PLM in the Europe region, you can decide to set up a BTP Subaccount in US10 – Virginia.

Latency

Some examples are based on global ping statistics, which may vary over time and can help you with an indication to pick the right subaccount for cross-consumption.

Note: This is not an SAP official statement, you may use this for reference only.

I hope this gives you a preview of how you can plan your activations for all LOBs to ensure you have the maximum benefits from SAP AI Services.

If you have any further questions on the SAP AI Services, you can refer to the official help page or reach us at SAP_AI_RIG@sap.com.

Regards,

Nagesh Caparthy

entry.title: How to find the volumes for a subassembly produced in the last 12 & 6 months
entry.content: How to find the volumes for a subassembly produced in the last 12 & 6 months

entry.title: QM inspection type 01 active but user wants to post the stock in to unrestricted creation of PO
entry.content:
Hello QM Gurus,

I am new to QM, could you please help me with below requirement.. QM module is active and ins ty 01 active for material.. but user selecting stock type as unrestricted while creating PO. But stock posting into QI. Can you help with better way to meet requirement without deactivting QM 01 inspection type for material

Thank you in advance 

Vskm

entry.title: Issues with Quick Confirm in Perform Maintenance Jobs
entry.content:
Hi,

First post so sorry if I post in wrong place.

We have migrated maintenance plans into SAP. Each maintenance plan have an Item with Task List. All Task List have operations with estimated work in hours and amount of personal needed.

If we try to do Quick Confirm in Perform Maintenance Jobs and we are not assigned to the Order/Operation, the page never stops loading. It would be nice with some error message.

A bigger issue is that in most cases when we are assigned to the Order and Operation and press Quick Confirm, we in the first step get message "Remaining Effort=NaN HR".
In next step we get "Property ActualWorkEffort at offset 181 has invalid value NaN".
Can someone guide me what to look for?

To open each operation in Perform Maintenance Job, press Start and confirm Work Done from there is not an option.

Thanks in advance!

Regards, Anders

entry.title: SSO Usage in the SAP Business ByDesign Mobile
entry.content:
You would like to know if the SSO is supported in the SAP Business ByDesign Mobile.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SSO Usage in the SAP Business ByDesign Mobile
entry.content:
Currently, this is the expected system behavior.

You can download the app in your work profile if your company allows it. However, Single Sign-On (SSO) won't function within your work profile or even if you download the app in your personal space.

Additionally, the app utilizes the Cordova plugin in-app browser, which means it cannot be opened with other browsers.

 

If you wish to access it via a browser, we have a 'mobilclient.html' URL available, which is:

https://myXXXXXXXX.sapbydesign.com/sap/public/ap/ui/repository/SAP_UI/HTMLOBERON5/mobileClient.html

Please change the XXXXXX to the Tenant URL accordingly.

entry.title: How to create a curriculum with item A and following item B with retraining?
entry.content:
Hello,
we need to implement the following scenario with a curriculum:

An employee must complete Item A within 365 days of the assignment date. The employee should then automatically be assigned item B. The employee must then complete Element B every two years.

For Item B, we have already stated that Item A is a prerequisite for Item B.

But how can I display the automatic assignment of items with dependencies in a curriculum?

Do you have any ideas?

Regards

Guido

entry.title: Re: Audit trail to track old and new values on table, HANA DB level
entry.content: One possibility: CREATE TRIGGER to insert changes into a custom table. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4fe29514fd584807ac9f2a04f6754767/20d5a65575191014946db96aaedbef5b.html

The RAP service is created using generate repository object and deployed as Fiori list report. Is it possible to have the inline edit and inline create for this autogenrated RAP report. 

Thanks,

Gayathri

This is even confirmed by the respective consultant of SAP. So it would be cool, if you point even to those limitations.

Thank you.

Best regards

Tobias

entry.title: Re: How to consolidate rows in a LOOP with same PO number and PO Line item
entry.content: AT CHANGE is obsolete. AT NEW is prone to errors so I consider it as obsolete. Choose the classic algorithm (if new value <> previous value then...)

entry.title: Re: Query manager yellow arrow for docnum from QIPF
entry.content:
This is the query:

SELECT T0.[CardCode], T0.[SuppName], T2.[WhsCode],
FORMAT(sum(T2.[Quantity]), '#,##0') as Qty,
FORMAT(SUM(T2.[TtlCostLC]), '#,##0.00') as 'Landed Cost', 
T0.[DocDate], T0.[DocNum], T0.[DocEntry]
FROM OIPF T0 
INNER JOIN IPF1 T2 on T0.DocEntry = T2.DocEntry
INNER JOIN OITM T3 ON T2.ItemCode = T3.ItemCode
INNER JOIN OITB T4 On T3.ItmsGrpCod = T4.ItmsGrpCod
WHERE (T0.[DocDate] >= [%1] and T0.[DocDate] <= [%2])
and (T0.[CardCode] >= [%3] and T0.[CardCode] <= [%4])
and T4.[ItmsGrpNam]  <> ''                                  
GROUP BY T0.[CardCode], T0.[SuppName], T2.[WhsCode], T0.[DocDate], T0.[DocNum], T0.[DocEntry]

When I click the yellow arrow on the Internal Number (T0.[DocEntry]), I will be linked to the correct document

entry.title: Re: ME5A enhancement for new field
entry.content:
In ME5A Add Custom Filed in ALV O/P.

To implement the desired functionality, you'll need to follow these steps:

Add the Desire Field:

In the structure MEREP_OUTTAB_EBANOV, add the new field to the include CI_EBANDB.

This might involve enhancing the structure to accommodate the new field.

Create Implicit Enhancement:

Go to the report RM06BA00.
Navigate to the subroutine Daten_lesen.
Find the end of the subroutine and create an implicit enhancement point named ZENH_ME5A_CUST_FIELD.
Modify the Desired Field:

Within the implicit enhancement, loop through the internal table it_eban.
Populate your new field based on your logic.

Here’s a conceptual example of how the enhancement might look:

FORM daten_lesen.
" Existing code...

" Implicit enhancement point
LOOP AT it_eban INTO DATA(wa_eban).
" Populate your desired field here
wa_eban-desired_field = "Your logic to populate the field".
MODIFY it_eban FROM wa_eban.
ENDLOOP.

" Existing code...
ENDFORM.

Make sure to replace "Your logic to populate the field" with the actual logic you want to use to fill your new field.

This approach will allow you to add your desired field and populate it as required in the report.

@Former Member wrote:

Hi Experts,

 

I have a requirement to add a custom field in tcode ME5A. I have searched and found some answer. ME5A Report - Request for Help | SCN

I have appended my new field in CI_EBANDB, but then how can I populate my new field? I need to use q query to populate the field.

But where here can I find any enhancement on how to populate the my new field in RM06BA00? Thanks

entry.title: Re: Poland - Sales billing
entry.content: Thanks for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts.

entry.title: Re: Need Help: SAP GUI restart after press on a customize GUI Status
entry.content: Reinstall SAP GUI latest version (8.xx) on the laptop. If it happens again, start the SAP GUI trace and send the file to the SAP Support for investigation. There is an SAP note which gives the details.

entry.title: Re: Numeric Point
entry.content:
Hi Lothar

Yeah I was testing this out, but I don't like this - requires I have a dimension with only 1 value for all the data sources.

Also it means I will have that value standing in the top left of the widget 😞 Can't hide it.

But thanks

 

BR

Nick

entry.title: Assembly order process - WBS structure
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently working on a change request for the assembly order process. I've completed all the necessary configurations and set up the standard template for the WBS structure. Upon saving the sales order, the project is being created successfully.

However, the issue I'm facing is that only the project definition, level-one WBS, and the network header are being generated, and the WBS structure up to level 3 is not being created.

Since we are not using activities, I haven’t allocated any WBS elements to activities in the standard network. I suspect this might be the reason for the incomplete WBS structure.

Could anyone advise on generating the full WBS structure in the assembly order process without using network activities?

Thanks 

Praveen

entry.title: Re: Introduction to SAP Customer Onboarding and Embedded Launch Activities for RISE with SAP

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • Women
    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • First generation college students
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
    • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I’m thrilled about this opportunity and eager to contribute. While I’m new to SAP, I’ve worked with other accounting systems and am confident in my ability to learn and excel. My goal is to become a skilled SAP consultant and join the professional community. I’m excited to start my training journey and see where it takes me. Please send the the registration link. 

Thank you so much!

entry.title: SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM - Need to be run on Solution Manager?
entry.content:
Good Day

Can you run ALM readiness check on an ECC system without Solution Manager? Basically do you need Solution Manager to run ALM readiness checks?

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM - Need to be ru...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM supports the analysis of SAP Solution Manager 7.2 systems.

If you are either considering or planning to move your SAP Solution Manager 7.2 system completely or partially to SAP Cloud ALM. As a result, you can analyze your existing SAP Solution Manager system with the SAP Readiness Check tool for SAP Cloud ALM, to help scope and plan your project.

It does not make sense to run it on an ECC system. 

see ##3236443 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Build process automation

entry.title: Re: Deploying App into SAP Build Work Zone / Fiori Launchpad in BTP
entry.content: wonderful blog, thanks for sharing excellent content..! @Hari89

entry.title: Re: Deploying App into SAP Build Work Zone / Fiori Launchpad in BTP
entry.content: Thanks for sharing @Hari89 ! Nice blog

entry.title: Re: Details of Write/Read Options and Value - Calling LDA...
entry.content:
Hi 

Please find the details for `LDAP_OPT_RESTART` and `LDAP_OPT_ERROR_NUMBER`:

LDAP_OPT_RESTART (Option Number: 9)
Meaning: This option controls whether the LDAP client should automatically retry an operation after a transient failure, such as a network timeout.
Functionality:
If enabled, the client attempts to reconnect and retry the operation if it encounters specific errors (like network interruptions).
This is useful for improving application resilience in environments with unstable connections.
Recommended Values:
`0`: Disable auto-restart (default behavior).
`1`: Enable auto-restart.

LDAP_OPT_ERROR_NUMBER (Option Number: 49)
Meaning: This option allows the client to retrieve the last error code that occurred during an LDAP operation.
Functionality:
After performing an LDAP operation (e.g., search, bind), you can check this option to get the integer error code representing the type of error encountered.
This is particularly useful for debugging and handling specific error scenarios in your application logic.
Usage: Typically, you call the function to retrieve the error number after an operation fails, and it does not have a setting value since it is read-only.

entry.title: Re: Consuming Data from Datasphere to Azure Data Factory via ODBC
entry.content:
Hello @vignesh3027 

Thanks a lot for the detailed and very helpful Blog 😉

As you might guess... there is one question remaining regarding the setup...

Imagine, the data you would like to transfer is the same, which is used for reporting purposes in SAC. That means you have a Fact view which protects the data via Data Access Controls.

How can you enable the database user to read "all" data from the view (or the analytic model, if you use this as source)? Is there a connection you can create from the database user to the user tables, created for DAC (which is usually the e-mail address)?

Thanks, Martin

entry.title: Re: How to register delivery dates for supplier responses...
entry.content: Better than this please use the PO API directly to update delivery dates which is the 3rd option of my reply which will also provide you the same functionality. I am not sure whether this app is of help related to Data Exchange exist or not, please raise OSS with SAP for that.

entry.title: Hi I need sample fs document for sap mm buniness scnerio.
Re: HU and Packaging Material Type in EWM S/4
entry.content: YEs , Packaging material type will always be 1 to 1 mapped with HU Type

entry.title: Re: COFC Period Posting issue for Work Order
entry.content:
Hello Preethi,
since it is not possible to post to a blocked period, you must specify the posting date for these periods in CAT9. Here you enter a value from the currently opened period as the posting date or as the cancellation date. /See picture)
Best regards
Marcus

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
@David_Chaviano I have completed Exploring SAP Cloud ERP. I have strong interest in SAP. I I consider it as penetrating in all walks of life in future and expect that huge number of industries will adopt and implement SAP. In this regard the certified SAP resources in various regions will become assets and valuable resources so I am interested to get certified in at least 03 areas in SAP as I have previous experience in various areas project management, Cloud implementation, Procurement and HR. I want to get maximum benefit from SAP generous offer of free attempt and want to learn maximum about SAP and to become expert in various SAP modules. 

Never stop learning, you stop learning and your progress and prosperity stops.

Thankyou SAP for providing this opportunity

entry.title: SAP EWM Stuck Queue - QM processing currently only possible for integrated warehouses
entry.content:
Hi Experts, we need your help here Once we goods receipt the quality management enabled products in warehouse the Inspection lot will be created. In mean time we are facing the Stuck queue called "QM processing currently only possible for integrated warehouses" this queue is carrying the Goods receipt info back to s4 , However, it is getting stuck and there is no goods movement completion was posted back in ERP.

May i know what the root cause for this would be?

EWM - Quality Management EWM - Interfaces EWM - Goods Movement

In Slipstrean Engine, the camera application for creating an attachment doesn't trigger the auto focus and the photo is blurred. What should I do ?

entry.title: Re: Goods Movement Types
entry.content:
Hello @Wenny 

In cloud there is access for OMJJ transaction, hence there is no central place to check for movement types, however refer to below of my old note which talks about other SSCUIs which can be referred to check for movement types.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002865146 

Best Regards,

Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: Fuzzy search for Business Partners in SAP S/4HANA on premise without TREX?
entry.content:
I have looked into SAP Fiori Apps and found the answer to my own question. The Fiori App "Manage Business Partner Master Data" allows doing a fuzzy search and fulfills the business requirement.
Link to Fiori Apps Library: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library (ondemand.com)

entry.title: Re: Please correct the quantity: Quantity on asset 1000 9...

Henrike

entry.title: #Coe#BUMA Custom Logic BAdI / Extensibility for Service Performer in Purchase Requisition
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a BAdI or custom logic extensibility for Service Performer in Purchase Requisition? Currently, Service Performer is only available in MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ITEM for Purchase Order.

Thank you,

Jen

entry.title: Re: “I now have SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. Do I still need a data warehouse?”
entry.content:
Hello @FreekKeijzer 

very interesting Blog... had fun while reading it. 😉

So far, I also had one more item on your list:

5. Number of reporting users

Don't you think it matters (at least in bigger companies), if you have ~100 or more users who are using reports in parallel at the beginning or end of the month, and affect the system, CPU, processes? There is the well-known "Whitepaper" of SAP regarding embedded BW, with max 20% of S4 data volume... but it affects CPU and processes...

However, regarding eBW. As long there is no end of life of it, in e.g. S4 HANA 2030, it might also one option to use this to include small amounts of external data.

I think you mainly focused on S4 tables only, but what do you think of the eBW capabilities in terms of embedded analytics?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Query manager yellow arrow for docnum from QIPF
entry.content:
Hi,

The query is ok but you need to check document numbering for Landed Cost. You may defined same document number for multi period (restart numbering).

For example:

2017: 1 >>> 9999

2024: 1 >>> 9999

In this case, It is better to use Internal Number instead of Document Number for Drill Down.

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.title: Re: SAP PE Session 'Conversion to SAP S/4 HANA and RISE with SAP - in one Go!' (September 19, 2024)

entry.title: Setup Approval Process for Sales Order in S4Hana Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to seek some help in setting up the approval process for Sales Order with a Net Value Condition. I've tried but the approval process doesn't kick in when the order reached or exceed that limit.

This is what I've setup so far in the system.

1st, i define the approval request in the configurations.

2nd, i setup the workflow for sales order with a net amount condition.

3rd, i created a custom logic for the workflow.

I've also checked to make sure the workflow scenario is activated.

However, my test Sales Order didn't trigger the approval process that I've setup.

 

May i ask what have i missed or could it be my setup is wrong?

Thank you very much for all guidance.

Best Regards,

Dan

entry.title: Re: How to consolidate rows in a LOOP with same PO number...

Otherwise it might be, I don't understand what you are trying to do.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: How to create new fields for journal entries mass upl...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question!

For the general procedure, can you please refer to the following document:

Upload General Journal Entries: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/49d0f15801b640639e801c36e2936597.html?locale=en-US

How to add fields: Adding Field Columns to Template
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/41f88d19c3b64b1db1c2aa80165d9494.html?locale=en-US

Use extensibility features:
App Extensibility: Upload General Journal Entries
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/d9fb8c4f76ef4277bad9b836fe86f2bb.html?version=2408.VAL

I have also forwarded the question to our experts.

Kind regards,

Henrike
